programmes.show_description: Gripping docuseries that explores ten murder cases from multiple perspectives. When a nine-year-old girl vanishes, suspicion falls on her troubled teenage neighbour. (Ep1/10)

Rachel schemes to make a handsome client ask her out on a date. Chandler's jealousy causes problems with his girlfriend.

programmes.show_description: Holly takes some frogspawn home from class to look after, but she gets a shock when it turns into tadpoles by the next morning. (VS)
Numbers: Peppa and her friends learn how to count by playing games at Madame Gazelle's playschool.
Peppa and her family are trying to get to the playground, but Mr Bull's roadworks are in the way.
Fluff discovers the power of sound, when an empty yarn cone gives his little voice a BIG impact! (Ep23)
Squiggle wants to play with his ball and searches for some friends to play with. Magnus and Dotty are happy to join in on the fun.
...Big Adventures! In Brazil, Thomas meets two wood-burning engines who've never travelled beyond their eucalyptus railway. Thomas agrees to swap jobs with them for the day.
...Normanski. Norman practices his magic act for the talent show, but his assistant Mandy is better at magic than he is.
Peppa and her friends are playing hide and seek, but it is Freddy Fox with his super smelling nose who is the best at playing that game.
Paddington has a fall and loses his climbing confidence; without climbing he doesn't feel like himself at all.
While building marble runs, Pip takes all the most exciting looking pieces, but Posy's simpler design works much better. They decide to trade pieces and create one mega marble run.
Encased in a huge chocolate bunny, Humdinger is carried away by hungry raccoons and ends up floating in Adventure Bay with a curious dolphin.
...Sparkle Sign. Pop star Sierra Sparkle is moving to Builder Cove! But when the pups plan a surprise greeting, Speed Meister makes a plan of his own.
Peppa and George are in for a treat when they embark on a camping trip to the seaside with Granny and Grandpa.
While Mummy and Daddy Pig attend a refresher antenatal class, Peppa and George join Miss Rabbit's Sibling School where they learn about what to expect when their new sibling arrives.
When Grandma gives Tim a hat she made herself, Tim is super excited, but unfortunately it keeps getting in the way.
A fish has gone missing from Grandad's pond and his plastic 'scare' heron appears to have come to life! Stan and Gran suspect something fishy is going on!
Chickaletta is left driving the train.
Milo and his pals are happy to step in to teach Miss Stern's evening-class students science, music and art, but sadly, not everyone responds to their teaching methods.
Holly is on a camping trip with Ben and his parents, but they are soon joined by annoying Mr Gnome, who is always hungry and singing silly songs.
Animated antics of a lovable piglet. Daddy Pig causes chaos when he improves Rebecca and Richard Rabbit's marble run game and the marble rolls into the lounge.
Odo and Doodle pretend to be 'Inspector Oodle' in The Inspector. Odo and Doodle even award the camp a Golden Acorn trophy.
Join Kiera as she discovers all about the wonderful world of jazz dancing with the help of some Milkshakers. (Ep5)
Join Kemi for a story where humans think 'Yetis don't exist'. When Iggy the Yeti hears this news, he sets off on a human search with his Yeti friends to help change their minds!

Matt looks at the big news stories developing during the day and offers help and advice to save you money and make your lives a little easier.
Raw, unfiltered, authentic and personal, Vanessa Feltz wants you to tell her what's on your mind. Fiery discussions, a good giggle and plenty of afternoon gossip!

programmes.show_name: New: Building the Impossible with Rob Bell
programmes.show_description: Rob sails to the furthest point of Britain to visit the Impossible Lighthouse at Muckle Flugga, forged in one of the most inhospitable spots in the North Atlantic.

programmes.show_name: Secrets of the Sun with Dara O Briain
programmes.show_description: Exploring our vital relationship with the Sun, from sun-worship and monuments such as Stonehenge, to how the Sun was formed and where its ferocious power originates. (Ep1/2)

programmes.show_name: Secrets of the Sun with Dara O Briain
programmes.show_description: Dara explores the darker side of the Sun, from the dangers of skin cancer to solar flares and the 'Bermuda Triangle of Space'. Plus, the world's largest solar furnace. (Ep2/2)

The Mixmups go on an Upside Down Adventure but, when the Lucky Loover Bird gets stuck on the ceiling, they need to find another way to get her down.
Help the PAW Patrol solve games, puzzles, and brainteasers in these mini-missions. (Ep3)
...Barrier Reef. Mrs Kangaroo takes everyone on an amazing adventure under the sea in her submarine to see the Great Barrier Reef!
The family's Australian holiday is coming to an end, but they still have time to play with a boomerang and make friends with Mr Wallaby.
When the fairies wake up to find that their wands are not working, the kingdom has to cope without any help from magic for the entire day.
As Thomas and Diesel race each other across Sodor, they take things a bit too far by putting an important delivery at risk for a simple competition.
Norman poses as an extra-terrestrial to make a science trip on the Pontypandy Flyer more interesting - which it certainly turns out to be!
Tim tries out some new rocket boots which soon fly out of control.
Mummy Pig is baking an apple pie, so Daddy Pig suggests Peppa and George play a game of looking for things.
Madame Gazelle shows the playgroup how cozy poetry and wooly socks can be.
Pip and Posy are decorating pancakes when Posy suggests they create pancake portraits of each other. It's great fun until they have different ideas about the best way to do it.
On Wintery Woohoo Day, Scotty wants to give a food present to his bird friend, but the Woohoos can't find it. They discover that ptarmigans' change their feathers' colour in winter. (Ep44)
Paddington accidentally destroys a squirrel's winter nut store, so he invites the wild animal to live indoors. But the squirrel invites all his friends...
Judy gets a toy spy kit, and Mrs Bird starts acting suspiciously, so the children and Paddington decide to follow her using the spy gadgets in the box.
...Tigers. When the Turbots fall into a cave inside a volcano, the pups parachute into action.
The Cadets visit Grace Lane Vets for a day of animal care, giving a leopard tortoise a health check, and help look after a little lamb called Brittany.
When a tarantula's unusual 'pet' goes missing, Sam and Kit team up with Wally Bungler to scour the jungle for the lost frog!
SpongeBob gets a pair of long pants, but they change his life in unexpected ways.
Larry the Lobster opens the doors of his new gym to the residents of Bikini Bottom for the first time, and SpongeBob becomes a loyal customer.

From lawnmower racing to weather reporting, Susan's Devon trip is full of surprises, including a Bridgerton-inspired carriage ride, Victorian railways, and a surf therapy session.

programmes.show_name: Ben Fogle: New Lives in the Wild
programmes.show_description: Ben heads to an island at the edge of the world on Norway's arctic coastline, and meets Elena, a former Middle East peacekeeper who has created her dream home here.

programmes.show_name: Ben Fogle: New Lives in the Wild
programmes.show_description: On the island of Koh Samui, Ben meets entrepreneur Niall who traded in a high-flying career for the beaches of Thailand to help street dogs who, in turn, have rescued him.
Frenchie Bingo is stopping Alan from finding romance. Can Graeme Hall help Alan find love? And Cockerpoo Molly cries at the top of her voice. Sleep deprived mum Amy is at her wit's end.
Graeme Hall tackles seemingly immoveable 60-kilo mountain dog Oakley whose obstinance leads to traffic jams when he plonks himself in the middle of the road.
...Royals with Rev Richard Coles & Emily Andrews. Richard and Emily answer another round of viewer questions and take a look at what is going on in the lives of the Windsors.
Animal patients in this episode include a calf with a suspected hernia, a dog with a lump, a mouse who needs an eyeball removed, and an alpaca called Voldermort.

programmes.show_name: Beyond The Yorkshire Farm: Reuben & Clive
programmes.show_description: Reuben and the team take on the challenges of winter during his third season in business. Mining for clay is a new challenge, and Niall's pond dig is hit by the cold. (Ep3/4)
...Reuben & Clive. With spring approaching, Clive's busy preparing for lambing, and Reuben and his mates go to a vintage tractor fair where a classic Leyland tipper catches Reuben's eye. (Ep4/4)
National and international news.

programmes.show_name: Birds of a Feather: 30 Years of Laughs
programmes.show_description: A celebration of the iconic comedy, revisiting favourite moments, taking a look behind the scenes and featuring insight from stars including Lesley Joseph and Linda Robson.

programmes.show_name: Lip-Reading the Royals: What Are They Really Saying?
programmes.show_description: What do the royal family really say when they think nobody is listening? We reveal what experts believe was said when the microphones are off and the stakes are high.

programmes.show_name: New: The Windsor Castle Fire: Minute by Minute
programmes.show_description: Featuring evocative and dramatic archive, and interviews with firefighters and royal staff, this documentary recalls when Queen Elizabeth II's family home went up in flames.
Jan Leeming narrates the headlines of 1988, when N.W.A. created the blueprint for gangsta rap, Edwina Currie caused a storm in an egg-cup, and the Channel Tunnel got underway.
